dialogos.texto_en: You guys are searching for souvenirs here, huh?
Oh, big bro!\nDid you find something good?
Hey, hey, slow down, 'kay.\nI haven't even found any fitting souvenirs yet.
Raiha-chan is really enthusiastic.\nIt seems like we might find something good.
There are a lot of food shops around here, huh.
Oh, yes.\nI was curious, how many different flavors\nof chinsuko biscuits are there?
What now, all of a sudden?
Well, the shop we went into earlier\nhad an astonishing variety.\nRight, Raiha-chan?
Yeah, there were too many to count.
Plain, salty, vanilla, brown sugar...\nThere were even some covered with chocolate.
There was also a flavor with Okinawan peppers.\nI wonder how spicy they are.
I might not be able to eat that.\nIf I'm getting it as a souvenir,\nI prefer something sweet.
If you want to get an Okinawan sweet, besides chinsuko,\nthe sweet potato tarts are also famous.\nIt seems like there are many varieties of those too.
We're looking for something memorable, right?\nAre we really going to go with food?
We won't do that.\nIt'll just disappear after we eat it, you know?
Good, at least you're sensible about that.
What do you take me for, Uesugi-kun?!
It's just that every souvenir shop sells food,\nso it's more noticeable.
Well, that's true.\nIt's a classic souvenir, after all.
A matching memento for all of us%p-2;――\n%p;I wonder what would work?
Maybe something practical, huh?
If it's practical,\nthen we'll use it every day, right?
Then, if we use it too much,\nit might break.
Right, that would be sad if it broke.
Considering that...\nmaybe some sort of decoration would be better?
Speaking of which, in the shop we went into earlier,\nthere were small shisa figurines, right?\nMaybe something like that would be good.
That might be a good idea.\nThose shisa had expressive and cute faces.
The expressions were different?
Yes, they were all unique.\nEach one might have been handmade.
It would be nice to take home the ones we like.
Well, we've talked about a lot of things,\nbut we don't have to decide on anything right now.\nWe can just keep it in the back of our mind.
We're going to different places\ntomorrow and the day after.\nWe might stumble upon something good.
It's hard to decide once you start thinking about it.\nHonestly, I hadn't thought it through\nwhen I brought it up. Sorry about that...
There's no need for you to apologize, Uesugi-kun.\nThe idea of getting souvenirs from our trip\nis a wonderful suggestion.
Yeah, that's right, big bro!\nLet's praise ourselves for being able to make thoughtful \nsuggestions!
O-okay.
This is so much fun! It'll become a great memory too.
Ah, you're right.
Well, we've talked about a lot of things,\nbut we don't have to decide on anything right now.\nWe can just keep it in the back of our mind.
We're going to different places\ntomorrow and the day after.\nWe might stumble upon something good.
It'll remind us of our trip each time we use it.\nIsn't that better than just keeping it on display?
So, everyday items can trigger your memory, huh?
Good point, big bro.
Well, I can't deny the possibility\nof accidentally breaking the souvenir.\nWe just have to be careful.
That's right,\nlet's try to choose things that won't break easily.
I still can't think of what to get, though.
Haha, the bar has been raised, right?
Well, we've talked about a lot of things,\nbut we don't have to decide on anything right now.\nWe can just keep it in the back of our mind.
We're going to different places\ntomorrow and the day after.\nWe might stumble upon something good.
That's right, our trip is far from over.
It really feels like a dream,\ndoesn't it? Is it really possible to have this much fun?
It's all good.\nAfter all, we invited you because we wanted to see\nyou happy, Raiha-chan.
Ehehe, thank you very much.
Besides, Raiha-chan is like family to us.\nRight, Uesugi-kun?
W-well, um...
That's right, when Ichika-san and big bro\nget married, we'll be family anyway.
R-Raiha?!\nDon't say that so bluntly,\nit's bad for my heart!
You know, it would be great if you proposed\nduring this trip. With the Okinawa sea in the background. \nSaying to her 'Please marry me!' How lovely that'll sound.
I see, that's a good idea.
Even you, Itsuki... Don't tease me...
Oh, I didn't intend to tease you.
Huh?
If you plan on taking away our dear big sister,\nthen be prepared for the consequences.
J-just kidding. I'm sorry for bringing up\nsuch a strange topic out of nowhere!
No, it's not strange.\nI understand your feelings.
Yes.\nPlease be strong, Uesugi-kun.
